title: paulownia tomentosa var. pallida
intro: Paulownia tomentosa var. pallida, also known as the white or pale-leaved empress tree, is a deciduous tree native to China that belongs to the bignonaceae family. The leaves, bark, and roots of the Paulownia tomentosa var. pallida plant are used in traditional medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its medicinal properties are associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs and respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. The herbal preparations of Paulownia tomentosa var. pallida include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions in traditional Chinese medicine.
